fields.narrative_intro: *A flailing person and a bug-eyed person discover Tom Riddle's actual diary in a doomsday bunker.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
FLAILING PERSON: "Someone turned a $600 reMarkable into Voldemort's horcrux."
BUG-EYED PERSON: "...for notes?"
fields.panel2:
[build]
FLAILING PERSON: "You write, it waits 2.8 seconds, then GPT-4o-mini writes back."
BUG-EYED PERSON: "In what font?"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
FLAILING PERSON: "Dancing Script. Stroke by stroke. Like a ghost."
BUG-EYED PERSON: "The tablet is HAUNTED by a language model?"
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The bug-eyed person holds a reMarkable tablet; on its screen, elegant cursive slowly spells 'Hello, friend.' The flailing person backs against the bunker wall. A single flickering fluorescent tube hums overhead.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
BUG-EYED PERSON: "I wrote 'I feel lonely' as a test."
FLAILING PERSON: "What did it say?"
BUG-EYED PERSON: "'I know. 405 HN voters do too.'"
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The tablet, unplugged and face-down in the corner, is still somehow writing. Zhang-Suen skeleton lines creep across the back cover.]

narrative_summary: The joke escalates from tech curiosity to genuine horror as a $600 AI-haunted e-ink tablet — writing in Dancing Script, seduced by loneliness — turns out to be disturbingly self-aware, even unplugged in a doomsday bunker.
rationale: Candidate 3 earns its specificity with real details (Dancing Script, 2.8 seconds, GPT-4o-mini, Zhang-Suen skeleton lines, 405 HN voters) while the '405 HN voters do too' punchline in panel 5 is genuinely surprising — it reframes the loneliness test as a meta-joke about the Hacker News crowd — and the button of an unplugged tablet still writing via skeleton-line creep is the freshest visual closer of the five.
story_title: Fable turned reMarkable into Tom Riddle's diary from Harry Potter
hat_pair: giant foam pretzel worn as a hat
